name: France
full: France — RTE, EDF & Bpifrance Deep-Tech Funding
sub: France invests €5B+ annually in energy R&D through Bpifrance. RTE and EDF partner with 100+ startups. CRE regulatory sandbox accelerates AI pilots. France 2030 supports €300M/yr in climate-tech deeptech.
desc: RTE operates 100,000+ km of transmission with explicit AI/forecasting roadmap. EDF Pulse Awards fund 50+ startups/yr across innovation areas. Bpifrance manages €300M/yr France 2030 Deep-Tech fund explicitly targeting AI, DeepTech, and ClimaTech. CRE (energy regulator) runs regulatory sandbox for AI pilots. ADEME provides €400M+ annually for energy transitions.

studies.label: RTE Innovation & Partnerships
studies.text:
What it doesRTE operates 100,000+ km French transmission grid. Runs open innovation programme with 100+ active startup partnerships. Explicit focus on forecasting, grid automation, and congestion management. €5.5B annual capex with AI integration mandate. Direct vendor partnerships, no nationality restriction.Why it mattersLargest single European grid operator by capex. AI for congestion forecasting is named priority.
RTE Innovation
studies.budget: Est. €100K–€1M per startup pilot (from €5.5B annual capex)
studies.deadline: Rolling — 100+ active startup partnerships. Direct approaches accepted year-round via rte-france.com.
studies.budgetBasis: RTE does not publish per-startup pilot budgets. Estimated from RTE's €5.5B annual capex, 100+ active startup partnerships, and comparable European TSO pilot engagement scales.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents can engage directly — no nationality restriction. RTE's 100+ startup partnerships and explicit AI congestion forecasting priority align well with Grid Agents' queue management technology.

relevance.text: R&D Roadmap 2025-2028 ('CAP R&D'): €50M+/yr, ~200 engineers, 9 research programmes. Key themes: digital substations, asset monitoring, grid-forming technology, offshore wind cable R&D, and climate resilience. Also runs RTE Smart Innovation Challenge via Agorize. 100+ active startup partnerships.

applicationRoute.step: Present grid automation and forecasting solution
applicationRoute.detail: Focus on congestion management and queue optimisation
Direct commercial engagement with technology deployment pathway

studies.label: EDF Pulse Awards
studies.text:
What it doesEDF's international startup programme funding 50+ startups annually. €50M+ investment portfolio. Explicit support for grid modernisation, AI, and renewable integration. Equity and partnership options.Why it mattersAccess to Europe's largest utility (180M customers across 10 countries).
EDF Pulse Awards
studies.deadline: Annual awards cycle. Applications typically open spring each year. Watch edf.fr/pulse for 2026 dates.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents can apply internationally — no nationality restriction. AI-powered grid automation platform is relevant to EDF's grid modernisation themes. 50+ startups funded annually.

relevance.text: 2026 edition applications closed Dec 2025, Grand Final June 2026. Five categories: Health-Safety, Electrification, Energy Flexibility, Operational Efficiency, Environmental Adaptability. €15K per winner plus EDF Pulse Connect support (lab testing, business model, end-user access). Access to 180M customers.

applicationRoute.step: Apply via EDF Pulse Awards portal

applicationRoute.detail: Evaluated on innovation impact and scalability

studies.label: Bpifrance France 2030 Deep-Tech
studies.text:
What it doesBpifrance (French development bank) administers France 2030 Deep-Tech fund: €300M/yr targeting AI, quantum, biotech, and climate innovation. Grants and equity financing available. Two-tier support: early-stage (€200K-€500K) and scale-up (€5M+).RequirementsFrench company registration (SARL/SAS, €500 + 2-3 weeks). Alternatively, partnership with French company acceptable.
Bpifrance AI Pioneers
studies.deadline: AI Pioneers call: Phase 1 deadline 2 June 2026 (moved up). Phase 2 deadline 9 June 2026. Agile 3-phase structure.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a French entity (SARL/SAS, €500 + 2-3 weeks) or French partner. AI and ClimaTech positioning fits France 2030 Deep-Tech criteria. Phase 1 deadline 2 June 2026.

relevance.text: AI Pioneers call open — Phase 1 deadline 2 June 2026, Phase 2 deadline 9 June 2026. First 23 laureates unveiled Feb 2026. Backed by €10M public funding. Bpifrance has deployed €10B for AI ecosystem. Ecological transition is an explicitly named sector.

applicationRoute.detail: €500 registration + 2-3 weeks — or identify French partner

applicationRoute.step: Apply via Bpifrance AI Pioneers call

studies.label: CRE Regulatory Sandbox
studies.hint: No funding — regulatory cover for AI pilots
studies.text:
What it doesFrench energy regulator (CRE) operates regulatory sandbox for AI-assisted grid operations testing. Provides comfort letters and derogations. Identical to UK Ofgem sandbox in principle. Open to international companies with French partner.
CRE Regulatory Sandbox
studies.deadline: Rolling — submit via demarches-simplifiees.fr. 6-8 week initial assessment. Pilots run 12-24 months.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a French partner (e.g. RTE or EDF). AI-assisted connection application review would benefit from regulatory sandbox cover for French market entry. Similar to Ofgem sandbox approach.

relevance.text: Active with 30 ongoing projects (May 2025). Notable: Reflex Project making 600-800 MW capacity available without waiting for connection plans across ~50 source stations (2025-2027). Rolling applications since Nov 2022. AI-assisted grid connection review is exactly the type of innovation this sandbox supports.

applicationRoute.detail: Comfort letters and derogations for 12-24 month pilot

studies.label: ADEME Energy Transition Funding
studies.text:
What it doesFrench Environment & Energy Management Agency. €400M+ annual budget for energy transitions, including grid modernisation, distributed generation, and smart systems. Grants (50-80% coverage) and low-interest loans available.
ADEME Funding
studies.deadline: Multiple calls per year. 2026 Theses call closed 2 March 2026. Building research and energy innovation calls ongoing — check agirpourlatransition.ademe.fr.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a French entity or significant French presence. AI grid automation is eligible but ADEME's focus is broader energy transitions, not specifically grid connection queues.

relevance.text: Current open calls include local electricity production & consumption (deadline Dec 2026), hydrogen production, and building innovation. Broader France 2030 calls cover decarbonisation and renewables. Grid digitalisation eligible but ADEME's focus is broader energy transitions, not specifically grid connection queues.

applicationRoute.detail: Entity or partnership with French organisation

studies.label: Enedis Innovation (France's Primary DSO)
studies.text:
What it doesEnedis manages 95% of France's electricity distribution network. Runs dedicated R&D and innovation programme. 35M Linky smart meters deployed providing grid data. 46 MW of local flexibility services across 30 departments. Innovation themes: smart grid/IoT, digital grid observability, fraud detection, capacity assessment, and SF6-free equipment (no new SF6 24kV switchboards since 2021).Why it mattersOverwhelmingly dominant French DSO. Major programmes include the €250M Marseille Network programme for urban grid resilience.
Enedis R&D Programme
studies.deadline: Rolling — innovation partnerships negotiated on individual basis.
studies.budgetBasis: Enedis does not publish per-partnership budgets. Estimated from Enedis R&D programme scope and comparable European DSO innovation partnerships.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would likely need French presence or partnership for Enedis engagement. Enedis manages 95% of French distribution — dominant DSO with dedicated innovation programme.

relevance.text: 35M Linky smart meters deployed, 46 MW flexibility services across 30 departments, €250M Marseille Network programme. Innovation focus on digital grid observability, capacity assessment, and fraud detection. 95% of French distribution — overwhelmingly dominant DSO with dedicated R&D programme.

applicationRoute.detail: Via Enedis R&D programme or industry events
Focus on digital observability, capacity assessment, or connection processing
Technology deployment across French distribution network

faq.q: Can we approach RTE without a French entity?
faq.a: Yes. RTE operates 100+ startup partnerships with no nationality restriction on direct vendor partnerships. However, you'll need French representation/contact for engagement.

faq.q: How does France 2030 relate to Bpifrance grants?
faq.a: France 2030 is the broader €300B national plan; Bpifrance administers the implementation. For startups, apply directly to Bpifrance's AI Pioneers call (France 2030 Deep-Tech fund, €300M/yr).

faq.q: What's the CRE regulatory sandbox approval timeline?
faq.a: 6-8 weeks for initial assessment. Once approved, pilots can run 12-24 months with regulatory cover and derogation letters.

faq.q: How much capital does it cost to register a French SARL?
faq.a: €500 registration cost + 2-3 weeks. Minimum capital: €1 (symbolic). Much simpler than German GmbH or Spanish setup.
No — you can partner with a French company and apply as a consortium, or register a French entity. Both routes are acceptable.

note: France offers €5B+ in energy R&D funding with the lowest company registration barrier in Europe (€500 SARL). RTE + EDF partnerships don't require local entity. Bpifrance AI Pioneers (€300M/yr Deep-Tech fund) unlocks massive AI and ClimaTech funding.
